item.title: New York City
item.description: Some of these photos were taken in New York city when I visited it in October for an AI forum. I wanted to take pictures in NYC for so long and felt this was as good a time as any. So, carried my old-ass camera around uptown and midtown manhattan, and took most of the photos.

item.title: Graffiti in Pittsburgh through 35mm
item.description: Early fall 2017, I went on a search for graffiti in Pittsburgh and found a lot! Took a bunch of snaps and developed them on Ilford HP5 35mm. To get most of these I had to bike around Pittsburgh (mostly in Lawrenceville and Oakland). Never knew Pittsburgh had so much street art (some might call it vandalism though).

item.title: Research
item.description: Check out news page for latest updates. Publications Conference Publications Contrasting Exploration in Parameter and Action Space: A Zeroth Order Optimization Perspective Anirudh Vemula, Wen Sun, J. Andrew Bagnell AISTATS 2019 (arxiv, code) Acceptance rate: 32.4% Social Attention: Modeling Attention in Human Crowds Anirudh Vemula, Katharina Muelling, Jean Oh ICRA 2018 (arxiv) Acceptance rate: 40.6% Modeling Cooperative Navigation in Dense Human Crowds Anirudh Vemula, Katharina Muelling, Jean Oh ICRA 2017 (arxiv)

item.title: Code
item.description: Most of my code is open sourced under GPL v3.0 license. Implementations of my papers and other related works can be found at my personal or lab’s github profiles. The following are a few highlights: Contrasting Exploration in Parameter and Action Space Exploration in Action Space Predictive uncertainty using deep ensembles Path Planning in Dynamic Environments using Adaptive Dimensionality Transition-based RRT* Anytime D* Depixelizing Pixel Art

item.title: About
item.description: Anirudh is a graduate student in the Robotics Institute at Carnegie Mellon University. He works with Maxim Likhachev and Drew Bagnell on research areas at the intersection of planning and learning. Anirudh tries to make robots move, grasp, think and plan. He fails often but every once in a while, he stumbles onto something. When not doing aforementioned, he can be seen biking - he wants to complete the GAP hiking - he is adept at avoiding spider webs climbing - he knows when to rotate his hips shooting and developing 35mm film - he uses a 1977 Pentax K1000 To know more about him, you can check out his CV and latest updates.

item.title: News
item.description: (Jan 2019) I was awarded the inaugral Amazon Go PhD Fellowship, 2019 (Jan 2019) I will be working with Vladlen Koltun at the Intelligent Systems Lab, Intel, Santa Clara over the summer of 2019 (Dec 2018) Our paper on why random search beats state-of-the-art policy gradient approaches got accepted at AISTATS 2019 (Nov 2018) A preliminary version of our paper is being presented at the Deep RL Workshop, NeurIPS 2018

item.title: Planner Ensemble for Adaptive Motion Planning
